summary: The Docker build failed because a `wget` command in the Dockerfile's `nydus` build stage received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error when attempting to download the nydus release tarball from GitHub. This caused the `buildx bake` command to fail with a non-zero exit code.
root_cause: During the Docker build, the RUN step at Dockerfile:336 executed `wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz` and received `HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error` from GitHub, causing the process to exit with code 1 and the entire build to fail.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: This is the direct error from wget showing a transient network/gateway failure when downloading from GitHub.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The wget failure caused the Dockerfile RUN step to exit with code 1, failing the build step.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: This is the top-level GitHub Actions error confirming the buildx bake command failed due to the wget network error.
Identifies the exact Dockerfile line (336) where the failure occurred.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient network failure (HTTP 502 from GitHub). If the issue persists, consider adding retry logic to the wget command in Dockerfile:336 (e.g., `wget --tries=3`) or mirroring the nydus release artifact to avoid direct GitHub dependency during builds.

summary: The CI job failed because the functional test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed after 3 retries. The test attempts to install a package from GitHub via HTTPS, but the connection to github.com repeatedly returned 502 errors, causing pip to raise an OSError after exhausting retries.
root_cause: Transient network failure: HTTPS requests to github.com returned too many 502 responses. The test `tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` tried to install `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip` but received `HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))` on all 3 attempts (including reruns).

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the actual pip error shown in the test's captured stderr, indicating a transient network failure (502 Bad Gateway) when trying to reach github.com.
The test was retried 3 times (3 RERUN entries), confirming the failure was consistent and not a one-off fluke, but still caused by the same network issue each time.
This is the final test failure summary, showing only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, confirming the failure is isolated to this network-dependent test.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed in the entire suite, and it was the GitHub-network-dependent test, strongly indicating a transient network issue rather than a code bug.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient network failure (GitHub returning 502 errors). Alternatively, consider marking `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` with a network-flaky marker or adding retry logic at the test level to tolerate intermittent 502 responses from github.com.

args.pattern: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnection

summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `"https://my-org.registry.example.com"` (no trailing slash) but received `"https://my-org.registry.example.com/"` (with a trailing slash), indicating that `pnpm config get` now appends a trailing slash to scoped registry URLs.
root_cause: The test at test/config/get.ts:32 asserts that `pnpm config get @my-org:registry` returns `"https://my-org.registry.example.com"` (no trailing slash), but the actual output is `"https://my-org.registry.example.com/"` (with a trailing slash). The behavior of `pnpm config get` changed to include a trailing slash on registry URLs, causing the strict `toBe` equality check to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc
evidence.reason: Identifies the exact failing test suite and test case name.
Shows the exact assertion mismatch: expected no trailing slash, received with trailing slash.
The test code at line 32 uses strict equality (`toBe`) against the URL without a trailing slash.
Pinpoints the exact file and line number of the failing assertion.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test suite and one test failed in the entire run.
Confirms the Jest run exited with status 1 due to the test failure, causing the CI job to fail.

suggested_fix: Update the test assertion in `test/config/get.ts` at line 32 to expect the trailing slash: change `toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com')` to `toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`, or alternatively fix the `pnpm config get` implementation to strip the trailing slash from registry URLs before outputting them.
